content_blocks.body_md:
Saccharin was the first artificial sweetener, discovered by accident in 1879
by a chemist who noticed his dinner tasted sweet after a day in the lab. Around
three hundred times sweeter than sugar, it carried a cancer warning for decades
before the science walked that back.
